author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Luke x. 16, ‘He that rejecteth you rejecteth me.’

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Luke x. 16, ‘He that rejecteth you rejecteth me.’

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What our Lord Jesus Crist at that time spake to His disciples was put in writing, and prepared for us to hear.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so we have heard His words.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what profit would it be to us if He were seen, and were not heard?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And now it is no hurt, that He is not seen, and yet is heard.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He saith then, “He that despiseth you, despiseth Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If to the Apostles only He said, “He that despiseth you, despiseth Me;” do ye despise us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if His word reach to us, and He hath called us, and set us in their place, see that ye despise not us, lest the wrong ye shall do unto us reach to Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if ye fear not us, fear Him who said, “He that despiseth you, despiseth Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But why do we, who are unwilling to be despised by you, speak to you, except that we may have joy of your good conversation?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let your good works be the solace of our perils.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Live well, that ye may not die ill.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And in these words which I have spoken, “Live well, that ye may not die ill,” do not think of those who it may be have lived evilly, and have died in their beds; and the pomp of their funeral has been displayed, and they have been laid in costly coffins, in sepulchres prepared with exceeding beauty and labour; nor because each one of you perhaps is saying, “I should wish so to die,” do ye think that it is a vain thing I have chosen to say; when I said that I would that ye should live well, that ye may not die ill?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: On the other hand, the case of some one, it may be, occurs to you, who has both lived well, and according to the opinion of men has died ill; perhaps he has died from the fall of a house, has died by shipwreck, has died by wild beasts; and each carnal man is saying in his heart, “What good is it to live well?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See this man has so lived, and in this wise has he died.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Return therefore to your heart;” and if ye are faithful ones, ye will find Christ there; He speaketh to you there.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I cry aloud, but He in silence giveth more instruction.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I speak by the sound of words; He speaketh within by the fear of the thoughts.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: May He then engraft my word in your heart; for I have taken upon me to say, “Live well, that ye may not die ill.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See, for faith is in your hearts, and Christ dwelleth there, and it is His place to teach what I desire to give utterance to.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Remember that rich and that poor man in the Gospel; “the rich man clothed in purple and fine linen,” and crammed with daily feastings; and the poor man “lying before” the rich man’s gate, hungry, and looking for “the crumbs from his table, full of sores, licked” by “dogs.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Remember, I say; and whence do ye remember, but because Christ is there in your hearts?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Tell me, what have ye asked Him within, and what hath He answered.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he goes on to say, “It came to pass that that poor man died, and was carried by the Angels into Abraham’s bosom.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The rich man also died, and was buried in hell.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And being in torments he lifted up his eyes, and saw Lazarus resting in Abraham’s bosom.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then he cried, saying, Father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus that he may dip his finger in water, and drop it on my tongue, for I am tormented in this flame.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Proud in the world, in hell a beggar!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that poor man did attain to his crumbs; but the other attained not to the drop of water.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of these two then, tell me, which died well, and which died ill?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not ask the eyes, return to the heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if ye ask the eyes, they will answer you falsely.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For vastly splendid, and disguised with much worldly show, are the honours which could be paid to that rich man in his death.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What crowds of mourning slaves and handmaids might there be! what pompous train of dependants! what splendid funeral obsequies! what costliness of burial!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I suppose he was overwhelmed with spices.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What shall we say then, Brethren, that he died well, or died ill?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If ye ask the eyes, he died very well; if ye enquire of your inner Master, he died most ill.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then those haughty men who keep their own goods to themselves, and bestow none of them upon the poor, die in this way; how do they die who plunder the goods of others?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore have I said with true reason, “Live well, that ye die not ill,” that ye die not as that rich man died.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nothing proves an evil death, but the time after death.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: On the other hand, look at that poor man; not with the eyes, for so ye will err; let faith look at him, let the heart see him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Set him before your eyes lying on the ground, “full of sores, and the dogs” coming and “licking his sores.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now when ye recall him before your eyes in this guise, immediately ye loathe him, ye turn your face away, and stop your nostrils: see then with the eyes of the heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “He died, and was carried by the Angels into Abraham’s bosom.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The rich man’s family was seen bewailing him; the Angels were not seen rejoicing.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then did Abraham answer the rich man?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Son, remember that thou in thy lifetime receivedst good things.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou thoughtest nothing good, but what thou hadst in this life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast received them; but those days are past; and thou hast lost the whole; and thou hast remained behind to be tormented in hell.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Opportune then was it, Brethren, that those words should be spoken to you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Have respect unto the poor, whether lying on the ground, or walking; have respect unto the poor, do good works.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye who are wont so to do, do it still and ye who are not wont to do so, do it now.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the number of those who do good works increase; since the number of the faithful increases also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye do not yet see how great is the good ye do; for so the husbandman also sees not the crop when he sows, but he trusts the ground.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore dost thou not trust God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Our harvest will come.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Think, that we are busy in travail now, are working in travail now, but sure to receive, as it is written, “They went on and wept as they cast their seed; but they shall surely come with exultation, bringing their sheaves with them.”
